slides.title: SQL vs NoSQL Database Architectures
leftPoints: рҹҸ—пёҸ Structured schema with tables and relations
рҹ”’ ACID compliance for data integrity
рҹ”Қ Complex queries with JOIN operations
рҹҺҜ Best for: Banking, e-commerce, ERP systems
rightPoints: рҹҡҖ Fast read/write for simple queries
рҹ“Ҡ Various types: Document, Key-Value, Graph
рҹҺҜ Best for: Social media, IoT, real-time analytics

note.text: Modern applications often use both - SQL for transactions, NoSQL for scalability

slides.title: Docker Container Architecture
slides.caption: Container isolation and resource management
slides.content:
рҹҸ—пёҸ Container Technology Revolution

Docker containers package applications with all dependencies, ensuring consistent behavior across environments from development to production.

  • вҡЎ Lightweight - Shares host OS kernel, minimal overhead
  • рҹ”’ Isolation - Process and filesystem separation
  • рҹ“Ұ Portability - Run anywhere with Docker runtime
  • рҹ”„ Version Control - Image layers and tags
  • рҹҸ—пёҸ Microservices - Perfect for distributed architectures

Containers start in milliseconds vs minutes for VMs

slides.title: Kubernetes Cluster Orchestration
slides.caption: Automated container deployment and scaling
slides.content:
рҹҺӣпёҸ Production-Grade Container Orchestration

Kubernetes automates deployment, scaling, and operations of application containers across clusters of hosts.

  • рҹ“Ҳ Auto-scaling - Horizontal and vertical pod scaling
  • рҹӣЎпёҸ Self-healing - Automatic restarts and replacements
  • рҹ”„ Rolling updates - Zero-downtime deployments
  • рҹ”Қ Service discovery - DNS-based load balancing
  • рҹ’ҫ Storage orchestration - Mount storage systems automatically

slides.title: CI/CD Pipeline Automation
slides.caption: Automated software delivery lifecycle
slides.content:
рҹҡҖ Continuous Integration & Deployment

Modern DevOps practices enable teams to deliver software rapidly and reliably through automated pipelines.

  • рҹ”Ё Continuous Integration - Merge code changes frequently
  • вң… Automated Testing - Unit, integration, and E2E tests
  • рҹ“Ұ Artifact Generation - Build and store deployable packages
  • рҹҡҖ Continuous Deployment - Automatic production releases
  • рҹ“Ҡ Monitoring - Real-time performance tracking

slides.title: Interactive Quiz Examples
slides.subtitle: Engage your audience with knowledge checks

slides.question: What does REST stand for in web API design?
slides.explanation: REST stands for Representational State Transfer. It's an architectural style that uses stateless communication and standard HTTP methods. RESTful APIs are resource-based and support caching, layering, and uniform interfaces.

slides.question: Which data structure operates on LIFO (Last In, First Out) principle?
slides.explanation: A Stack operates on LIFO principle - the last element added is the first removed. Used in function call stacks, browser history, undo operations, and expression evaluation. Time complexity: O(1) for push/pop operations.

slides.question: What is the time complexity of binary search in a sorted array?
slides.explanation: Binary search has O(log n) complexity. Each iteration halves the search space. For 1 million elements, it takes only 20 comparisons (2^20 вүҲ 1 million). Much faster than linear search O(n).

slides.question: Which protocol provides secure communication over computer networks?
slides.explanation: SSL/TLS (Secure Sockets Layer/Transport Layer Security) provides encrypted communication, authentication, and data integrity. Used in HTTPS to secure web traffic, APIs, and sensitive data transmission.

slides.title: Git Feature Branch Workflow
slides.content:
Main Branch Timeline:

main:    Aв”Җв”Җв”ҖBв”Җв”Җв”ҖCв”Җв”Җв”ҖDв”Җв”Җв”ҖEв”Җв”Җв”ҖFв”Җв”Җв”ҖMв”Җв”Җв”ҖN
          \              /        \
           \            /          \
feature-1:  Gв”Җв”Җв”ҖHв”Җв”Җв”ҖIв”Җв”ҳ            \
                                    \
feature-2:                          Jв”Җв”Җв”ҖKв”Җв”Җв”ҖL


Workflow Steps:
1. рҹҸ—пёҸ  Create feature branch from main
2. рҹ’»  Make commits (G, H, I) on feature-1
3. рҹ”„  Regularly merge main into feature branch
4. рҹ“қ  Open Pull Request for code review
5. рҹ‘Җ  Team review and approval
6. вң…  Automated CI/CD pipeline runs
7. рҹҡҖ  Merge to main (M, N)
8. рҹ—‘пёҸ  Delete feature branch after merge

Best Practices:
вҖў Keep features small and focused
вҖў Write meaningful commit messages
вҖў Review code thoroughly before merging
вҖў Ensure all tests pass in CI pipeline

note.text: Feature branches enable parallel development while maintaining stable main branch

slides.title: Presentation Engine Evolution
rightPoints: рҹҸ—пёҸ Flexible grid and responsive layouts

slides.title: Building Modern Presentations
slides.caption: Create engaging technical presentations
slides.content:
рҹҺЁ Design Principles

Effective technical presentations combine clear information architecture with engaging visual design.

  • рҹҺҜ Audience Focus - Tailor content to technical level
  • рҹ“– Storytelling - Create narrative flow
  • рҹ”„ Interactivity - Engage with quizzes and polls
  • рҹ“Ҡ Data Visualization - Make complex data understandable
  • рҹҡҖ Performance - Fast loading and smooth animations
